action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 17's eval produced ic_mean=0.001143, far below the known best of 0.02045. The local test showed ic_mean=-0.004449. Analysis: predict.py's fallback logic checks for best.pt BEFORE best_512.pt. The workspace still contains an ancient best.pt from early iterations (3-epoch underfit model). When the ensemble manifest was deleted in iter 17, predict.py loaded this useless best.pt instead of the proven best_512.pt. Decision: (1) Delete the misleading best.pt and model_config.json, (2) update predict.py to prefer best_512.pt in the fallback path and add defensive defaults for input_dim, (3) run a quick diagnostic to inspect best_512 config and mean/std statistics, then run local predict.py to verify IC is restored before any official eval submission.
action.command:
rm -f checkpoints/best.pt checkpoints/model_config.json && python -c "
import json, numpy as np
cfg = json.load(open('checkpoints/best_512_config.json'))
print('best_512 config:', cfg)
m = np.load('checkpoints/train_mean.npy')
s = np.load('checkpoints/train_std.npy')
print('mean shape:', m.shape, 'std shape:', s.shape)
print('mean min/max:', m.min(), m.max())
print('std min/max:', s.min(), s.max())
" && python predict.py --data_path local_test_eval --checkpoint checkpoints --output local_test_eval/output.json
